topic: User-provided document review for an externally authored policy or contract
reader: internal reviewer who needs a grounded summary of what the document says and where it is risky
use_context: A document-review memo that treats the uploaded text as the primary object, cross-checks it against authoritative references, and surfaces ambiguity or missing context.
known_failure_modes: summarizing the document without checking its claims
treating user-provided assertions as verified fact
prohibited_overclaims: does not assume the document is accurate because it was provided by the user
does not rewrite the document's meaning
does not claim review completeness when references are missing

source_packets.title: User-provided source document
source_packets.summary: The provided document contains policy language, a few defined terms, and several statements that appear to require corroboration.

findings.statement: The document defines a key term narrowly, which may exclude related cases from its coverage.
findings.decision_note: Call out the narrow definition so the reader sees the scope effect.
failure_modes: assuming the term is broader than the text allows
misunderstandings: reading the defined term as if it included all adjacent cases

findings.statement: One section references another document that is not attached, making the obligation hard to finalize.
findings.decision_note: Mark this as an unresolved review gap unless the missing document is found.
failure_modes: treating an incomplete cross-reference as fully resolved
misunderstandings: assuming the referenced document says the obvious thing

source_packets.title: Authoritative cross-check source
source_packets.summary: Provides a reference point for checking whether the user-provided document's claims are consistent with the applicable authoritative text.

findings.statement: The authoritative text uses a different threshold than the user document implies.
findings.decision_note: This is the main reason to avoid copying the user document's phrasing uncritically.
failure_modes: treating the user document's threshold as authoritative
misunderstandings: assuming the two documents use the same threshold language

findings.statement: The authority leaves one edge case unstated, so review output should preserve that uncertainty.
findings.decision_note: Do not over-fill this gap in the summary.
failure_modes: inventing a clean answer where the source stays silent
